name: Ghormeh Sabzi
description: A classic Persian dish made with fresh herbs, tender beef, kidney beans, and dried limes, known for its rich, tangy, and aromatic flavor.
alt: A bowl of Ghormeh Sabzi, a traditional Persian herb stew with beef and kidney beans

instructions.value: In a large pot, sauté the chopped onion in a bit of oil until golden.
Add the cubed beef and turmeric, cooking until the meat is browned on all sides.
Add the chopped herbs and sauté for a few minutes until they wilt.
Pour in enough water to cover the ingredients, then add the dried limes.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for about 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the meat is tender.
Add the cooked kidney beans and continue to simmer for another 30 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste before serving.

name: Chelow
description: ََA fluffy Persian-style white rice with separated grains, often served with a crispy golden crust called tahdig.
alt: A dish of Chelow, traditional Persian steamed rice with tahdig

instructions.value: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear to remove excess starch.
Soak the rice in salted water for at least 30 minutes, then drain.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil, add salt, and cook the rice for about 5-7 minutes until it's partially cooked (al dente).
Drain the rice and rinse with cold water to stop the cooking process.
In a non-stick pot, heat oil or butter over medium heat. Add a layer of parboiled rice, then add a layer of potato slices or flatbread for tahdig.
Continue layering the remaining rice on top.
Cover the pot with a clean kitchen towel and place the lid on top to trap steam.
Cook on low heat for about 30-40 minutes until the rice is fully cooked and the tahdig is crispy and golden.
Serve the chelow with your choice of stew or grilled meat.

name: Joojeh Kabab
description: A popular Persian dish made with marinated chicken, grilled to juicy perfection with a tangy saffron flavor.
alt: A plate of Joojeh Kabab, Persian grilled chicken skewers with a golden saffron marinade

ingredients.value: a pinch of Saffron (dissolved in hot water)

instructions.value: In a bowl, combine the plain yogurt, lemon juice, dissolved saffron, minced garlic, grated onion, salt, and pepper. Mix well to create the marinade.
Add the chicken pieces to the marinade and coat them thoroughly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight for best flavor.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat. Thread the marinated chicken pieces onto skewers.
Grill the chicken skewers for about 10-15 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and has a nice char.
Serve the joojeh kabab with chelow (steamed rice) or flatbread, along with grilled tomatoes and fresh herbs.

name: Gheymeh
description: A hearty Persian stew made with tender beef, yellow split peas, dried limes, and a tomato-based sauce, often garnished with crispy fried potatoes.
alt: A bowl of Gheymeh, a traditional Persian stew with beef, yellow split peas, and dried limes

instructions.value: In a large pot, sauté the chopped onion in a bit of oil until golden.
Add the cubed beef and turmeric, cooking until the meat is browned on all sides.
Add the tomato paste and cook for a few minutes to develop the flavor.
Pour in enough water to cover the ingredients, then add the dried limes.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for about 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the meat is tender.
Add the cooked yellow split peas and continue to simmer for another 30 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste before serving. Garnish with crispy fried potatoes on top.

name: Khoresh-e Esfenaj va Alu
description: A traditional Persian stew made with spinach, tender meat, and sweet-sour prunes, creating a rich and comforting flavor.
alt: A bowl of Khoresh-e Esfenaj va Alu, a traditional Persian stew with spinach and prunes

instructions.value: Heat oil in a pot and sauté the chopped onion until golden.
Add turmeric and meat; cook until the meat is browned.
Pour in water, cover, and simmer for about 60 minutes until the meat is tender.
In a separate pan, sauté the spinach until wilted and reduced in volume.
Add the spinach and prunes to the pot.
Season with salt and pepper to taste before serving.
Add lemon juice (optional) for a slightly tangy flavor.

name: Kebab Tabei
description: A simple and juicy Persian-style minced meat kebab cooked in a pan instead of a grill, making it an easy homemade version of traditional kebab.
alt: A plate of Kebab Tabei, a Persian-style pan-cooked minced meat kebab

instructions.value: In a bowl, combine the minced meat with finely chopped onion, minced garlic, turmeric, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Mix well until all ingredients are evenly incorporated.
Divide the meat mixture into equal portions and shape each portion into a flat oval or round patty.
Heat a non-stick skillet or frying pan over medium heat. Place the patties in the pan and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until they are cooked through and have a nice browned crust.
Serve the kabab tabei with chelow (steamed rice) or flatbread, along with grilled tomatoes and fresh herbs.

name: Tahchin
description: َA famous Persian baked rice dish made with yogurt, eggs, saffron, and layered chicken or meat, known for its crispy golden crust and rich aroma.
alt: A slice of Tahchin, a Persian baked rice dish with a crispy golden crust and layers of chicken and saffron rice

ingredients.value: a pinch of Saffron (dissolved in hot water)

instructions.value: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear to remove excess starch.
Soak the rice in salted water for at least 30 minutes, then drain.
In a bowl, mix the plain yogurt, eggs, dissolved saffron, turmeric, salt, and pepper. Add the shredded chicken to the mixture and stir to combine.
In a non-stick pot or baking dish, spread a thin layer of oil or butter on the bottom. Add a layer of parboiled rice, then spread a layer of the chicken and yogurt mixture on top.
Continue layering the remaining rice and chicken mixture until all ingredients are used.
Cover the pot with a clean kitchen towel and place the lid on top to trap steam.
Cook on low heat for about 30-40 minutes until the rice is fully cooked and the bottom layer forms a crispy golden crust.
Alternatively, you can bake it in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for about 45-60 minutes until fully cooked and golden brown.
Serve tahchin with your choice of stew or grilled meat.

name: Kotlet
description: A crispy, golden fried patties made from minced meat, potatoes, and spices, commonly served as a quick and delicious Persian street-style meal.
alt: A plate of Kotlet, crispy fried patties made from minced meat and potatoes

instructions.value: Boil the potatoes until tender, then mash them and let them cool.
In a large bowl, combine the minced meat, mashed potatoes, finely chopped onions, minced garlic, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper. Mix well until all ingredients are evenly incorporated.
Shape the mixture into oval or round patties.
Heat oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Fry the patties in batches until they are golden brown and cooked through on both sides.
Drain the fried kotlet on paper towels to remove excess oil.
Serve hot with bread, fresh herbs, or a side of yogurt.

name: Kookoo Sabzi
description: A traditional Persian herb-based omelet made with fresh herbs and eggs, often served cold or warm as a light meal or appetizer.
alt: A plate of Kookoo Sabzi, a traditional Persian herb-based omelet

instructions.value: In a large bowl, beat the eggs and season with salt, pepper, and turmeric.
Add the chopped herbs, onions, and walnuts (if using) to the beaten eggs. Mix well to combine.
Heat oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Pour the egg and herb mixture into the skillet, spreading it out evenly.
Cook on medium-low heat until the bottom is set and golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Carefully flip the kookoo using a large plate or lid to help turn it over. Cook the other side until golden brown and fully set, about another 5-7 minutes.
Serve warm or at room temperature with bread or yogurt on the side.

name: Kookoo Sibzamini
description: A popular Persian dish made with mashed potatoes, eggs, and spices, pan-fried until crispy on the outside and soft inside.
alt: A plate of Kookoo Sibzamini, a Persian potato omelet with a crispy golden crust

instructions.value: In a large bowl, beat the eggs and season with salt, pepper, and paprika.
Add the mashed potatoes, onions, and garlic to the beaten eggs. Mix well to combine.
Heat oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Pour the egg and potato mixture into the skillet, spreading it out evenly.
Cook on medium-low heat until the bottom is set and golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Carefully flip the kookoo using a large plate or lid to help turn it over. Cook the other side until golden brown and fully set, about another 5-7 minutes.
Serve warm or at room temperature with bread or yogurt on the side.

name: Chelow Kebab
description: One of the most famous Persian dishes, consisting of fragrant saffron rice served with juicy grilled meat kebabs, often accompanied by grilled tomatoes.
alt: A plate of Chelow Kebab, Persian grilled meat kebabs served with saffron rice and grilled tomatoes

ingredients.value: a pinch of Saffron (dissolved in hot water)
Oil as required for frying or grilling

instructions.value: Prepare the kebab by mixing the minced meat or chicken with chopped onion, minced garlic, dissolved saffron, salt, and pepper. Shape the mixture into kebabs.
Grill or pan-fry the kebabs until they are cooked through and have a nice char.
Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear to remove excess starch.
Soak the rice in salted water for at least 30 minutes, then drain.
Cook the rice in boiling salted water until it's partially cooked (al dente), then drain and rinse with cold water.
In a non-stick pot, heat oil and add a layer of parboiled rice. Continue layering the remaining rice on top.
Cover the pot with a clean kitchen towel and place the lid on top to trap steam. Cook on low heat for about 30-40 minutes until the rice is fully cooked and has a crispy golden crust (tahdig).
Serve the chelow with the grilled kebabs, along with grilled tomatoes and fresh herbs.

name: Ash Reshteh
description: A hearty Persian soup made with herbs, beans, noodles, and topped with fried onions, garlic, and mint
alt: A bowl of Ash Reshteh, a traditional Persian soup with herbs, beans, and noodles

instructions.value: In a bowl, combine the plain yogurt, lemon juice, dissolved saffron, minced garlic, grated onion, salt, and pepper. Mix well to create the marinade.
Add the lamb chops to the marinade and coat them thoroughly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight for best flavor.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat. Grill the lamb chops for about 4-5 minutes on each side for medium-rare, or longer if you prefer them more well-done.
Serve the shishlik with chelow (steamed rice) or flatbread, along with grilled vegetables and fresh herbs.
